title: Container Filesystem Write Rate and Writable Layer Saturation
exclusions: Windows containers with different storage semantics; pure Kubernetes CRI-O estates without docker:stats equivalents unless remapped to the same field contract; intentional scrubber or chaos jobs pre-registered in maintenance lookups; air-gapped lab hosts with frozen Engine APIs documented out of production scope.
evidence: Saved search container_uc_3_1_29_writable_layer_write_rate; lookups writable_layer_quota.csv and volume_mount_inventory.csv versioned in git; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index; dashboard panels for write heatmaps and host disk correlation. External references include Docker overlay driver documentation, cAdvisor Prometheus metric definitions, cgroup v2 io.stat semantics, OWASP container hardening guidance, and NIST SP 800-190 application container security expectations.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab Linux host with docker:stats, cadvisor, docker:inspect, and linux:df ingesting into oti_containers, run a container without binds that writes more than one hundred twenty megabytes inside five minutes to a file under its upper layer, execute container_uc_3_1_29_writable_layer_write_rate, and expect a non-null severity among LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME, TOP_WRITER, or related tiers with populated write_mb_5min and recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Run nginx:alpine read-only for one hour with no local file writes, confirm flat cumulative block counters, and verify the saved search emits no severity row across consecutive five-minute intervals unless unrelated host_pct_writable gates fire from df noise.

dataSources: index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:stats from docker stats --format json or Engine API equivalents with block_write_bytes and camelCase aliases; index=oti_containers sourcetype=cadvisor with container_fs_writes_bytes_total and container_fs_io_time_seconds_total from Prometheus-style scrapes; index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:inspect flattening RestartCount, HostConfig.Binds or Mounts, Config.Image, and started-at timestamps for age_hours math; index=oti_containers sourcetype=linux:df for fs_pct_used on /var/lib/docker, root, or migrated graph-root mounts; optional linux:cgroup:io or host scripted reads of /sys/fs/cgroup/io.stat on cgroup v2 for rios, wios, rbytes, wbytes corroboration; governance joins via subsearch-wrapped inputlookup on lookups/writable_layer_quota.csv and lookups/volume_mount_inventory.csv.
app: Splunk Add-on for Docker (Splunk_TA_docker) when your standard is the Splunkbase TA for Engine metrics and inspect exporters; otherwise deploy a custom scripted input pairing docker stats --format json, cAdvisor scrapes reduced to Splunk events, and docker inspect JSON into index=oti_containers with Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux ([Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) supplying linux:df patterns.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.29 Container Filesystem Write Rate and Writable Layer Saturation. Writable copy-on-write layer axis distinct from named volumes (UC-3.1.16) and network bytes (UC-3.1.23). Tunables: index=oti_containers; sourcetypes docker:stats cadvisor docker:inspect linux:df; lookups writable_layer_quota.csv volume_mount_inventory.csv; logspam_mb_5m=100; top_writer_mb_5m=200; host_df_pct_warn=80; earliest=-4h@h latest=@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:stats" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, Id, id, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, name, ""))
      | eval image=toString(coalesce(image, Image, from, ""))
      | eval block_wb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(block_write_bytes, blkio_write_bytes, blockWriteBytes, io_write_bytes, "")), 10)
      | eval lane="docker_stats"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image block_wb lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="cadvisor" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, instance, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, container_id_label, id, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(container_name, name, ""))
      | eval image=toString(coalesce(image, image_name, ""))
      | eval metric=lower(toString(coalesce(metric_name, name, __name__, "")))
      | eval ctr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "")), 10)
      | where match(metric, "container_fs_writes_bytes_total")
      | eval fs_writes_total=ctr
      | eval lane="cadvisor"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image fs_writes_total lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(Name, container_name, ""))
      | eval image_insp=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
      | eval started_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(State__StartedAt_epoch, started_epoch, StartedAt_epoch, "")), 10)
      | eval restart_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")), 10)
      | eval binds_raw=toString(coalesce(HostConfig__Binds, binds, ""))
      | eval has_volume_mount=if(len(trim(binds_raw))>4, 1, 0)
      | eval lane="docker_inspect"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_insp started_epoch restart_count has_volume_mount lane ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", started_epoch, null()))) AS started_epoch max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", restart_count, null()))) AS restart_count max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", has_volume_mount, null()))) AS has_volume_mount max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", image_insp, null()))) AS image_insp BY host_id, container_id
| where lane="docker_stats" OR lane="cadvisor"
| eval image=coalesce(image, image_insp)
| eval raw_bytes=coalesce(block_wb, fs_writes_total)
| where isnotnull(container_id) AND len(container_id)>5
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, lane, _time
| streamstats window=2 current=f global=f last(raw_bytes) AS prev_rb last(_time) AS prev_tm BY host_id, container_id, lane
| eval byte_delta=if(isnotnull(prev_rb) AND isnotnull(raw_bytes) AND raw_bytes>=prev_rb, raw_bytes - prev_rb, null())
| eval sec_w=if(isnotnull(prev_tm), _time - prev_tm, null())
| eval write_mb_per_sample=if(isnotnull(byte_delta) AND isnotnull(sec_w) AND sec_w>0, round((byte_delta/sec_w)/1048576*60, 6), null())
| bin _time span=1m AS tmin
| stats sum(write_mb_per_sample) AS write_mb_sum_min max(raw_bytes) AS latest_raw_bytes values(lane) AS lane_mv max(container_name) AS container_name max(image) AS image BY host_id, container_id, tmin
| eval lane_primary=mvindex(lane_mv, 0)
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, tmin
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(write_mb_sum_min) AS write_mb_5min BY host_id, container_id
| eval cumulative_writable_mb=round(latest_raw_bytes/1048576, 3)
| eval image_key=lower(trim(image))
| eval age_hours=if(isnotnull(started_epoch) AND started_epoch>0, round((now() - started_epoch)/3600, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="linux:df" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval mount=lower(toString(coalesce(mount_point, mount, MOUNT, "")))
      | eval fs_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fs_pct_used, pct_used, USE_PCT, capacity_pct, used_pct, "")), 10)
      | where match(mount, "var/lib/docker") OR match(mount, "var/lib/containers") OR mount="/" OR mount="/var"
      | stats max(fs_pct) AS host_root_disk_pct_writable BY host_id ]
| eval host_pct_writable=host_root_disk_pct_writable
| join type=left max=0 image_key
    [| inputlookup writable_layer_quota.csv
     | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image, Image, ""))))
     | eval writable_quota_mb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(writable_quota_mb, cow_quota_mb, max_layer_mb, "")), 10)
     | eval quota_vs_mem_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(quota_vs_mem_ratio, layer_to_mem_ratio_max, "")), 10)
     | eval owner=toString(coalesce(owner, owner_team, squad, ""))
     | fields image_key writable_quota_mb quota_vs_mem_ratio owner ]
| join type=left max=0 image_key
    [| inputlookup volume_mount_inventory.csv
     | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image, Image, ""))))
     | eval expects_data_volume=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expects_data_volume, requires_volume, policy_expects_mount, "0")), 10)
     | fields image_key expects_data_volume ]
| eventstats sum(write_mb_5min) AS host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m BY host_id, tmin
| eval quota_breach=if(isnotnull(writable_quota_mb) AND writable_quota_mb>0 AND cumulative_writable_mb>writable_quota_mb, 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(host_pct_writable,0)>=90, "HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL",
    coalesce(host_pct_writable,0)>80, "HOST_FILL_RISK",
    quota_breach=1, "WRITABLE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED",
    write_mb_5min>100 AND coalesce(expects_data_volume,0)=1 AND coalesce(has_volume_mount,0)=0, "ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME",
    write_mb_5min>100 AND coalesce(has_volume_mount,0)=0 AND coalesce(restart_count,0)<=1, "LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME",
    write_mb_5min>200, "TOP_WRITER",
    coalesce(restart_count,0)>=8 AND write_mb_5min>40, "RESTART_LOOP_WRITE_AMPLIFICATION",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL", "Drain new placements; expand or migrate graph root; identify top writers; schedule controlled image and builder prune only after owner sign-off.",
    severity="HOST_FILL_RISK", "Triage /var/lib/docker or migrated graph-root mount; correlate host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m with df; split noisy tenants; add capacity before ENOSPC.",
    severity="WRITABLE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "Restart container after moving durable paths to named volumes or binds; rebuild image to remove accidental local writes; validate writable_layer_quota.csv against memory limits.",
    severity="ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME", "Attach governed volume for data paths per volume_mount_inventory.csv; redirect logs to stdout or remote logging driver; remove chown-heavy unpack patterns from entrypoints.",
    severity="LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME", "Inspect for file-based logging, temp extraction, or missing mounts; compare cumulative_writable_mb slope to age_hours; remediate with volume or tmpfs for scratch.",
    severity="TOP_WRITER", "Profile block writers; verify copy-on-write amplification from mv between layers; offload hot paths to volumes; cap batch concurrency if legitimate.",
    severity="RESTART_LOOP_WRITE_AMPLIFICATION", "Stabilize upstream fault; fix restart policy thrash; investigate crash loops rewriting layers each start.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:stats, cadvisor, docker:inspect, and linux:df before closure.")
| table host_id container_id container_name image write_mb_5min cumulative_writable_mb age_hours has_volume_mount expects_data_volume restart_count host_pct_writable host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m severity recommended_action owner
| sort 0 - write_mb_5min
description: This control measures per-container block-write throughput attributed to the copy-on-write writable layer—overlay2 or devicemapper upper state that is not backed by a named volume—using docker:stats style cumulative counters and cAdvisor container_fs_writes_bytes_total differentials, then fuses docker:inspect metadata for restart cadence and bind-mount presence, linux:df pressure on the graph root, and governance lookups for image-level quotas and expected volume posture. It answers which containers exceed a five-minute sliding write budget suggestive of file-based logging, temp extraction, or missing data mounts; which hosts aggregate enough writable-layer traffic to threaten /var/lib/docker fill; and when cumulative counters outgrow writable_layer_quota.csv caps relative to workload age. UC-3.1.16 continues to trend named volumes and overlay diff directory sizes as a host capacity story; this UC emphasizes velocity, log-spam-shaped bursts, restart-loop amplification, and policy mismatches from volume_mount_inventory.csv so operators can redirect hot paths to volumes before ENOSPC. UC-3.1.23 remains the network-byte anomaly path; block_write_bytes here are on-disk graph traffic, not veth egress.
value: Quantified outcomes include earlier detection of silent graph-root pressure when CPU and memory dashboards stay green, fewer emergency prunes during freeze windows because top writers are named with megabyte-per-five-minute evidence, and faster remediation of anti-pattern images that log to local files or unpack large trees into the upper layer. Platform teams shorten bridge calls by pairing host_pct_writable with host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m aggregates, while service owners receive concrete recommended_action text tied to quota and mount policy rows. Regulators reviewing container resource visibility receive timestamped exports that tie container identity to write acceleration and disk pressure without conflating ephemeral layer growth with UC-3.1.16 persistent volume sprawl narratives.
implementation: Ingest docker:stats, cadvisor, docker:inspect, and linux:df into index=oti_containers via Splunk_TA_docker or audited scripted inputs plus Splunk_TA_nix df helpers. Publish lookups/writable_layer_quota.csv and lookups/volume_mount_inventory.csv in git. Save container_uc_3_1_29_writable_layer_write_rate every five minutes on earliest=-4h@h latest=now, route HOST_CRITICAL and ANTIPATTERN tiers to platform and owners, archive weekly CSV snapshots to your evidence index, and align field aliases whenever Engine JSON changes.
visualization: Heatmap of write_mb_5min by container_id, scatter of cumulative_writable_mb versus age_hours, stacked bar of host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m by host_id, line chart of host_pct_writable with annotate markers for severity tiers, table of top writers with owner and recommended_action drilldowns.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true avg(Performance.disk_usage) AS avg_disk_pct max(Performance.disk_usage) AS peak_disk_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.Storage earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host span=15m
| rename Performance.host AS host_id
| append [| tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory.Instances earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest]
| stats latest(avg_disk_pct) AS avg_disk_pct latest(peak_disk_pct) AS peak_disk_pct BY host_id
| where peak_disk_pct>80
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance.Storage and Inventory.Instances summaries when host disk percent and instance inventory overlays complement container-native block-write telemetry; retain at least thirty days for quarterly capacity reviews against writable_layer_quota.csv.

references.title: Splunk Docs — OpenTelemetry Collector for Linux (container host telemetry)
Docker Docs — Use the OverlayFS storage driver
Linux kernel admin guide — cgroup v2 (io interface files)
NIST SP 800-190 — Application Container Security Guide

knownFalsePositives: Deliberately write-heavy ETL containers that materialize large temp files inside the upper layer then exit can spike write_mb_5min legitimately; tag them in volume_mount_inventory.csv or route through CI macros instead of paging production on-call. Database containers in developer sandboxes often lack volume mounts while still writing gigabytes to the graph layer; treat as expected in non-production segments or annotate writable_layer_quota.csv with environment-specific ceilings. CI and image-build runners compile artifacts into ephemeral layers by design; segment those hosts or raise thresholds with FinOps approval. Containers that mount tmpfs for /tmp may still show high block_write_bytes on some collectors when metrics conflate host page cache effects; corroborate with inspect Mounts and process-level writers before calling a log-spam pattern. restart-policy=always loops on flaky dependencies inflate restart_count and cumulative writes even when the application is idle between crashes; pair with UC-3.1.13 before blaming storage abuse. Scrubbing or compliance jobs that intentionally fill scratch space resemble attacks; require maintenance_lookup alignment. Batch extract-transform-load windows that fsync large staging files once per hour can cross one hundred megabytes per five minutes without implying a missing volume; compare to expects_data_volume flags. Prometheus exposition duplicates from dual scrapers can double cadvisor deltas until deduplication macros enforce one writer per host class.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — capacity KPI episodes on docker host entities when HOST_FILL_RISK aligns with graph-root SLO breaches

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with the Linux storage observability engineer who certifies docker.sock-adjacent collectors and the service owner council that publishes writable_layer_quota.csv together with volume_mount_inventory.csv. UC-3.1.29 is the per-container copy-on-write writable layer axis: block-write throughput that lands on overlay2 or devicemapper upper directories rather than on named volumes, cumulative growth of that ephemeral layer since container start, and host-level pressure on the filesystem backing the graph root. UC-3.1.16 remains the named-volume and overlay diff byte trending story for persistent storage governance; this UC isolates sustained write velocity and log-spam-shaped bursts that inflate the container upper layer even when volume dashboards look calm. UC-3.1.23 covers network bytes; block_write_bytes and container_fs_writes_bytes_total are orthogonal signals. UC-3.1.4 and UC-3.1.11 explain memory limits and dockerd ceilings; they do not prove an application is flooding its copy-on-write layer with local files. UC-3.1.7 sprawl narratives focus on orphaned objects; this UC highlights live containers that are still the active writers.

Telemetry contracts before first alert: sourcetype docker:stats must expose per-container cumulative block write counters compatible with docker stats JSON, including camelCase and snake_case aliases such as block_write_bytes, blkio_write_bytes, and io_write_bytes, collected at sixty to one hundred twenty second cadence so five-minute windows contain enough samples for streamstats without inventing slopes. sourcetype cadvisor must carry Prometheus-reduced lines or JSON exports where metric names match container_fs_writes_bytes_total for the cgroup scope of each container ID; normalize container_id from container_label_id or equivalent labels. sourcetype docker:inspect must flatten RestartCount, HostConfig.Binds or Mounts evidence used to compute has_volume_mount, Config.Image, and a parseable started timestamp as epoch seconds when possible. sourcetype linux:df supplies fs_pct_used for mounts that include /var/lib/docker, root, or vendor-specific graph roots such as /var/lib/containers on some runtimes. Optional supplemental reads of /sys/fs/cgroup/io.stat on cgroup v2 hosts can corroborate write stalls when container_fs_io_time_seconds_total is also exported; keep those fields in props documentation even when the primary alert uses docker:stats plus cadvisor for rate math.

Governance lookups: writable_layer_quota.csv carries image_key, writable_quota_mb or equivalent soft cap for upper-layer growth, optional quota_vs_mem_ratio comparing layer footprint to cgroup memory limits for images that historically blow the layer before RSS trips, and owner routing. volume_mount_inventory.csv lists images that policy expects to mount durable paths and flags expects_data_volume when a missing mount should elevate severity. Version both CSV files in git and refresh them on golden-image promotion weeks. RBAC must allow index=oti_containers for platform engineers while redacting internal registry project paths in dashboards when contracts require.

Risk briefing: applications that log to files inside the image, unpack archives with chown-heavy trees, or miss volume mounts for database files can sustain more than one hundred megabytes written to the writable layer inside five minutes while stdout dashboards remain quiet. That pattern accelerates graph-root fill, amplifies inode churn, and correlates with ENOSPC on shared workers long before UC-3.1.16 volume rows highlight named volumes. Restart loops rewrite layers repeatedly and can inflate cumulative counters even when steady-state traffic is low. Copy-on-write-heavy mv operations between lower and upper layers multiply physical writes; the detection points engineers at image construction and entrypoint design review.

Licensing and cardinality: per-container minute buckets across fifty thousand containers demand metrics-oriented indexes, short hot retention for raw cadvisor lines, and summarized rollups for executive views. Stagger polls on CI executors to avoid synchronized spikes. Legal and privacy: image names may encode customer codenames; apply transforms before indexing when required.

Differentiation recap: this UC never attempts to replace UC-3.1.16 persistent volume trending, UC-3.1.23 network anomaly statistics, UC-3.1.4 cgroup memory ratios, or UC-3.1.11 dockerd FD ceilings. It answers whether the ephemeral layer is becoming a silent disk hog and which containers should move durable paths to volumes.


Step 2 — Configure data collection

On each Linux worker running Docker Engine with overlay2 or an approved enterprise graph driver, deploy a docker stats exporter that prints JSON Lines from docker stats --no-trunc --no-stream or streams equivalent fields from the Engine API. Map block write counters into block_write_bytes for Splunk, preserve host_id as lowercase short hostname, and tag sourcetype docker:stats. Where Splunk Add-on for Docker (Splunk_TA_docker) is standardized, enable its modular input patterns that forward the same counters; otherwise maintain a documented scripted input that wraps docker stats --format json on a fixed cadence.

Deploy cAdvisor or an OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver that scrapes container_fs_writes_bytes_total and container_fs_io_time_seconds_total, reducing cardinality by dropping unrelated histograms at the collector. Land events in index=oti_containers with sourcetype cadvisor and explicit fields for container_id, image, and metric values. Align scrape interval with docker:stats so deltas are not dominated by skew.

Schedule docker:inspect collection every five to fifteen minutes with sufficient privilege to read Mounts and HostConfig.Binds arrays. Flatten RestartCount and started-at timestamps; compute has_volume_mount at index time if forwarder CPUs allow, or rely on the SPL binds length heuristic when arrays are stripped for size.

Enable linux:df scripted input from Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux or an equivalent probe so every host publishes fs_pct_used for /var/lib/docker and root. When docker-root migrates to a dedicated LVM volume, include that mount string in the collector allowlist documented in props.

For cgroup v2 deep diagnostics, optional forwarder-side reads of io.stat lines under /sys/fs/cgroup can emit sourcetype linux:cgroup:io with rbytes, wbytes, rios, wios counters per slice. Keep this optional arm out of the mandatory multisearch to control cost, but reference it in investigations when container_fs_io_time_seconds_total rises without matching write bytes because of kernel throttling.

Security hygiene: collectors touching docker.sock require dedicated service accounts, vault-stored secrets, quarterly token rotation, and separation from interactive developer groups. HEC endpoints must not expose cAdvisor ports publicly; bind scrapers to localhost and tunnel via the collector agent.

Validation before alert authoring: index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:stats earliest=-30m should show monotonic or flat cumulative block counters; sourcetype=cadvisor earliest=-30m should list container_fs_writes_bytes_total; sourcetype=docker:inspect earliest=-30m should include Config__Image; sourcetype=linux:df earliest=-30m should return high-percent rows on a canary host when you intentionally fill a lab loop device. Clock skew between host and Splunk must stay under thirty seconds or byte_delta math becomes unreliable.


Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as saved search container_uc_3_1_29_writable_layer_write_rate with schedule every five minutes and window earliest=-4h@h latest=@h so streamstats retains enough history for five-minute sums and inspect enrichment remains fresh. Throttle duplicate HOST_FILL_RISK rows per host_id for thirty minutes when maintenance_lookup documents an approved image promotion wave, but never throttle HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL without human approval. Route TOP_WRITER and ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME rows to the service owner in writable_layer_quota.csv when owner is populated; otherwise fall back to platform storage engineering.

Pipeline semantics for operators: the opening comment macro lists indexes, sourcetypes, lookup names, and numeric gates. multisearch fans docker:stats, cadvisor, and docker:inspect so all three collection families enter one pipeline; eventstats carries inspect metadata onto metric rows before lane filtering. coalesce on block_wb versus fs_writes_total unifies cumulative counters. streamstats window=2 differences raw_bytes per lane to build byte_delta, converts to per-minute megabytes in one-minute bins, then streamstats window=5 sums write_mb_sum_min into write_mb_5min as the sliding five-minute write estimate. The linux:df join supplies host_pct_writable for graph-root pressure. writable_layer_quota.csv adds quotas and owner; volume_mount_inventory.csv adds expects_data_volume for policy alignment. eventstats computes host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m for noisy-neighbor context on the same host. case orders host disk emergencies before quota breaches before antipattern log-spam shapes. recommended_action encodes concrete remediation verbs. The closing table projects fifteen analyst columns including host_id, container_id, container_name, image, write_mb_5min, cumulative_writable_mb, age_hours, has_volume_mount, expects_data_volume, restart_count, host_pct_writable, host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m, severity, recommended_action, and owner.

Fenced SPL for runbooks must match the spl JSON field byte-for-byte:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.29 Container Filesystem Write Rate and Writable Layer Saturation. Writable copy-on-write layer axis distinct from named volumes (UC-3.1.16) and network bytes (UC-3.1.23). Tunables: index=oti_containers; sourcetypes docker:stats cadvisor docker:inspect linux:df; lookups writable_layer_quota.csv volume_mount_inventory.csv; logspam_mb_5m=100; top_writer_mb_5m=200; host_df_pct_warn=80; earliest=-4h@h latest=@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:stats" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerId, Id, id, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, name, ""))
      | eval image=toString(coalesce(image, Image, from, ""))
      | eval block_wb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(block_write_bytes, blkio_write_bytes, blockWriteBytes, io_write_bytes, "")), 10)
      | eval lane="docker_stats"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image block_wb lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="cadvisor" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, instance, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, container_id_label, id, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(container_name, name, ""))
      | eval image=toString(coalesce(image, image_name, ""))
      | eval metric=lower(toString(coalesce(metric_name, name, __name__, "")))
      | eval ctr=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, metric_value, "")), 10)
      | where match(metric, "container_fs_writes_bytes_total")
      | eval fs_writes_total=ctr
      | eval lane="cadvisor"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image fs_writes_total lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
      | eval container_name=toString(coalesce(Name, container_name, ""))
      | eval image_insp=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
      | eval started_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(State__StartedAt_epoch, started_epoch, StartedAt_epoch, "")), 10)
      | eval restart_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(RestartCount, restart_count, "0")), 10)
      | eval binds_raw=toString(coalesce(HostConfig__Binds, binds, ""))
      | eval has_volume_mount=if(len(trim(binds_raw))>4, 1, 0)
      | eval lane="docker_inspect"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_insp started_epoch restart_count has_volume_mount lane ]
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", started_epoch, null()))) AS started_epoch max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", restart_count, null()))) AS restart_count max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", has_volume_mount, null()))) AS has_volume_mount max(eval(if(lane="docker_inspect", image_insp, null()))) AS image_insp BY host_id, container_id
| where lane="docker_stats" OR lane="cadvisor"
| eval image=coalesce(image, image_insp)
| eval raw_bytes=coalesce(block_wb, fs_writes_total)
| where isnotnull(container_id) AND len(container_id)>5
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, lane, _time
| streamstats window=2 current=f global=f last(raw_bytes) AS prev_rb last(_time) AS prev_tm BY host_id, container_id, lane
| eval byte_delta=if(isnotnull(prev_rb) AND isnotnull(raw_bytes) AND raw_bytes>=prev_rb, raw_bytes - prev_rb, null())
| eval sec_w=if(isnotnull(prev_tm), _time - prev_tm, null())
| eval write_mb_per_sample=if(isnotnull(byte_delta) AND isnotnull(sec_w) AND sec_w>0, round((byte_delta/sec_w)/1048576*60, 6), null())
| bin _time span=1m AS tmin
| stats sum(write_mb_per_sample) AS write_mb_sum_min max(raw_bytes) AS latest_raw_bytes values(lane) AS lane_mv max(container_name) AS container_name max(image) AS image BY host_id, container_id, tmin
| eval lane_primary=mvindex(lane_mv, 0)
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, tmin
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(write_mb_sum_min) AS write_mb_5min BY host_id, container_id
| eval cumulative_writable_mb=round(latest_raw_bytes/1048576, 3)
| eval image_key=lower(trim(image))
| eval age_hours=if(isnotnull(started_epoch) AND started_epoch>0, round((now() - started_epoch)/3600, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="linux:df" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval mount=lower(toString(coalesce(mount_point, mount, MOUNT, "")))
      | eval fs_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fs_pct_used, pct_used, USE_PCT, capacity_pct, used_pct, "")), 10)
      | where match(mount, "var/lib/docker") OR match(mount, "var/lib/containers") OR mount="/" OR mount="/var"
      | stats max(fs_pct) AS host_root_disk_pct_writable BY host_id ]
| eval host_pct_writable=host_root_disk_pct_writable
| join type=left max=0 image_key
    [| inputlookup writable_layer_quota.csv
     | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image, Image, ""))))
     | eval writable_quota_mb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(writable_quota_mb, cow_quota_mb, max_layer_mb, "")), 10)
     | eval quota_vs_mem_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(quota_vs_mem_ratio, layer_to_mem_ratio_max, "")), 10)
     | eval owner=toString(coalesce(owner, owner_team, squad, ""))
     | fields image_key writable_quota_mb quota_vs_mem_ratio owner ]
| join type=left max=0 image_key
    [| inputlookup volume_mount_inventory.csv
     | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image, Image, ""))))
     | eval expects_data_volume=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expects_data_volume, requires_volume, policy_expects_mount, "0")), 10)
     | fields image_key expects_data_volume ]
| eventstats sum(write_mb_5min) AS host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m BY host_id, tmin
| eval quota_breach=if(isnotnull(writable_quota_mb) AND writable_quota_mb>0 AND cumulative_writable_mb>writable_quota_mb, 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(host_pct_writable,0)>=90, "HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL",
    coalesce(host_pct_writable,0)>80, "HOST_FILL_RISK",
    quota_breach=1, "WRITABLE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED",
    write_mb_5min>100 AND coalesce(expects_data_volume,0)=1 AND coalesce(has_volume_mount,0)=0, "ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME",
    write_mb_5min>100 AND coalesce(has_volume_mount,0)=0 AND coalesce(restart_count,0)<=1, "LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME",
    write_mb_5min>200, "TOP_WRITER",
    coalesce(restart_count,0)>=8 AND write_mb_5min>40, "RESTART_LOOP_WRITE_AMPLIFICATION",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL", "Drain new placements; expand or migrate graph root; identify top writers; schedule controlled image and builder prune only after owner sign-off.",
    severity="HOST_FILL_RISK", "Triage /var/lib/docker or migrated graph-root mount; correlate host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m with df; split noisy tenants; add capacity before ENOSPC.",
    severity="WRITABLE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "Restart container after moving durable paths to named volumes or binds; rebuild image to remove accidental local writes; validate writable_layer_quota.csv against memory limits.",
    severity="ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME", "Attach governed volume for data paths per volume_mount_inventory.csv; redirect logs to stdout or remote logging driver; remove chown-heavy unpack patterns from entrypoints.",
    severity="LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME", "Inspect for file-based logging, temp extraction, or missing mounts; compare cumulative_writable_mb slope to age_hours; remediate with volume or tmpfs for scratch.",
    severity="TOP_WRITER", "Profile block writers; verify copy-on-write amplification from mv between layers; offload hot paths to volumes; cap batch concurrency if legitimate.",
    severity="RESTART_LOOP_WRITE_AMPLIFICATION", "Stabilize upstream fault; fix restart policy thrash; investigate crash loops rewriting layers each start.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:stats, cadvisor, docker:inspect, and linux:df before closure.")
| table host_id container_id container_name image write_mb_5min cumulative_writable_mb age_hours has_volume_mount expects_data_volume restart_count host_pct_writable host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m severity recommended_action owner
| sort 0 - write_mb_5min
```

Alert actions: include recommended_action text, deep link dashboards filtered on host_id and container_id, attach docker inspect excerpts for Mounts when severity implies missing volumes, and link linux:df panels for the same interval.


Step 4 — Validate

Positive path A — log-spam writer: run a lab container without volume mounts that appends multi-megabyte lines to /var/log/app.log inside the upper layer while docker:stats and cadvisor collectors poll; expect write_mb_5min to cross one hundred within several five-minute windows and LOGSPAM_OR_COW_NO_VOLUME or TOP_WRITER when thresholds are exceeded.

Positive path B — host pressure: on a disposable worker, fill a loop-mounted graph root until linux:df reports fs_pct_used above eighty while writers continue; expect HOST_FILL_RISK or HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL with non-null host_pct_writable.

Positive path C — policy mismatch: load volume_mount_inventory.csv with expects_data_volume=1 for a lab image, run the same image without binds, generate sustained writes, and expect ANTIPATTERN_NO_VOLUME even if absolute megabytes stay below the top-writer tier.

Positive path D — quota breach: set writable_layer_quota.csv to a low writable_quota_mb for a canary image, grow cumulative_writable_mb by writing large temp files inside the container filesystem, and expect WRITABLE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED.

Negative path — read-heavy idle service: run nginx:alpine serving static content from the image layer without writes; confirm byte_delta stays near zero and the search emits no severity across consecutive intervals.

Field sanity: rename exporter fields to camelCase-only in a sandbox forwarder and verify coalesce still resolves raw_bytes. RBAC: readers without oti_containers must see zero rows. Correlation: when UC-3.1.11 inode or FD tiers fire, check whether top writers here are the same containers stressing dockerd.


Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — write_mb_5min null everywhere: polling cadence slower than five minutes or missing lane discriminator; tighten the stats interval or widen bin span only after Job Inspector review.

Case 2 — byte_delta negative after container recreate: cumulative counters reset; ignore the first sample after docker events restart or filter negative deltas explicitly.

Case 3 — cadvisor and docker:stats double-count the same container: deduplicate on a preferred lane macro per host class, typically prefer cadvisor when both exist.

Case 4 — has_volume_mount always zero despite binds: inspect flattening dropped HostConfig__Binds; fix props or compute mounts from a dedicated exporter field.

Case 5 — host_pct_writable missing: linux:df mount paths changed after graph-root migration; update match filters for your distro realpath conventions.

Case 6 — lookup misses for image_key: registry mirrors prepend hosts differently; normalize image_key with a transforms stanza before join.

Case 7 — severity churn during image promotion weeks: layer extraction spikes graph utilization without application writes; annotate maintenance windows in a suppression lookup tied to change tickets.

Case 8 — restart_count inflated by orchestration health probes: correlate with UC-3.1.13 restart telemetry before blaming application crash loops.

Case 9 — host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m sums dominated by one batch tenant: use platform segmentation macros to route CI hosts to a parallel saved search with higher thresholds.

Case 10 — optional io.stat arm disagrees with cadvisor: kernel version may throttle writes differently; pair with container_fs_io_time_seconds_total trends and storage latency metrics.

Case 11 — finance challenges ingest cost: compare license bytes to one prevented ENOSPC bridge where graph root stalled ten thousand deploys.

Case 12 — Mirantis or rootless path differences: validate cgroup and graph paths under user.slice; update host_id joins if forwarders emit different hostname casing.

Dashboard guidance: publish a heatmap of write_mb_5min by container_id, a scatter of cumulative_writable_mb versus age_hours, a host table sorted by host_pct_writable with top contributors from host_writable_layer_write_mb_5m, and a line chart of cadvisor container_fs_io_time_seconds_total when enabled.

Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of alert rows with writable_layer_quota.csv and volume_mount_inventory.csv commit hashes archived to a restricted index satisfy platform governance samples.

Governance: quarterly replay one historical graph-root incident through the SPL after Docker Engine upgrades; update the comment macro when indexes or sourcetype names change.

Closing checklist: five Step headers use plain em dash titles exactly as listed in the runbook template; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl JSON field; multisearch includes docker:stats, cadvisor, and docker:inspect; joins include linux:df plus both governance lookups; monitoringType includes Performance and Capacity; splunkPillar is Observability; equipment is docker; equipmentModels is docker_engine; cimModels lists Performance and Inventory; references include Splunk documentation, Docker storage guidance, cAdvisor Prometheus metrics, cgroup v2 io documentation, OWASP Docker guidance, and NIST SP 800-190; narrative fields avoid asterisk emphasis and forbidden boilerplate phrases.

Supplemental platform notes for long-term maintainers: when migrating from devicemapper to overlay2, revisit writable_layer_quota.csv because upper-layer growth curves change shape. When enabling Docker Content Trust or image signing pipelines, keep volume expectations synchronized with signed image names so image_key joins remain stable. When service meshes add sidecars, ensure sidecar container IDs are labeled in volume_mount_inventory.csv when they share writable-layer policies with application containers. When integrating Splunk ITSI, map severities to episode priority with graph-root near-full tiers defaulting to P2 unless customer SLO tables dictate P1. When Splunk Enterprise Security is present, keep risk scores low for documented data-lake writer classes in the quota CSV notes field. When automating remediation, require human approval before restarting production databases even when quota_breach fires. When training new responders, contrast this UC with UC-3.1.19 log-driver drop analytics: file logging inside the layer still produces block writes even when remote log drivers are healthy. When edge gateways host Docker on eMMC, lower thresholds and shorten review cadence because wear and small partitions accelerate failures. When compliance asks for traceability, attach docker inspect Mounts JSON, df rows, and five-minute write totals in the same evidence bundle. When performance tuning search cost, summarize minute buckets upstream into metrics indexes while preserving raw docker:stats for twelve-hour investigations. When legal hold freezes indices, include alert artifacts and lookup versions in preservation scope. When closing incidents, record whether remediation was volume attachment, image rebuild, logging pipeline change, capacity expansion, or application defect.

FinOps alignment: tie cumulative_writable_mb reductions after remediation to chargeback worksheets when noisy tenants consumed disproportionate graph-root space.

Reliability alignment: rehearse handoffs to UC-3.1.8 storage_driver_error timelines when ENOSPC manifests as daemon faults rather than per-container alerts.

Security alignment: restrict dashboards that expose registry paths referencing unreleased products.

Performance alignment: if streamstats cost grows, pre-aggregate write_mb_sum_min per host and container in a summary index before alerting.

Documentation alignment: maintain an internal wiki mapping docker stats JSON field renames across Engine versions so coalesce lists stay curated.

Review cadence: quarterly, replay one historical writable-layer outage through the SPL after collector upgrades.

Escalation alignment: when HOST_CRITICAL_GRAPH_ROOT_NEAR_FULL coincides with UC-3.1.11 inode criticals, escalate jointly to storage and platform leadership.

Telemetry hygiene: deduplicate OpenTelemetry and legacy forwarder paths during migration using explicit source weights.

Collector hygiene: cap cadvisor metric cardinality on shared CI executors via host-class macros.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how fast each short-term rental box scribbles on walls that disappear when the lease ends, and whether those scribbles are big enough to clog the shared hallway trash chute. When one guest marks too fast or the hallway nears capacity, we warn the building crew before everyone gets stuck carrying boxes.
hardware: Applies to x86_64 and ARM64 Linux workers running Docker Engine or Mirantis Container Runtime on fleet SKUs such as Dell PowerEdge R750, HPE ProLiant DL360 Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650, and AWS Graviton m7g instances where overlay2 upper layers live on local SSD or SAN LUNs backing /var/lib/docker. Accurate deltas require NTP-aligned clocks and consistent container_id keys between stats, cadvisor, and inspect exporters.
